name: Roxatidine acetate
description: Roxatidine acetate is a specific and competitive H2 receptor antagonist. It is currently approved in South Africa under the tradename Roxit.
indication: For the treatment of disorders of the upper gastro-intestinal region that are due to an excess of hydrochloric acid in the gastric juice, i.e. duodenal ulcers, benign gastric ulcers. Also for prophylaxis of recurrent gastric and duodenal ulcers
mechanismOfAction: The H2 antagonists are competitive inhibitors of histamine at the parietal cell H2 receptor. They suppress the normal secretion of acid by parietal cells and the meal-stimulated secretion of acid. They accomplish this by two mechanisms: histamine released by ECL cells in the stomach is blocked from binding on parietal cell H2 receptors which stimulate acid secretion, and other substances that promote acid secretion (such as gastrin and acetylcholine) have a reduced effect on parietal cells when the H2 receptors are blocked.
pharmacodynamics: Roxatidine acetate suppresses the effect of histamine on the parietal cells of the stomach (H2-receptor antagonist). This suppressive action is dose-dependent. As a result, the production and secretion, particularly of gastric acid, are reduced. Roxatidine acetate has no antiandrogenic effects and does not influence drug-metabolizing enzymes in the liver.

interactions.name: Dasatinib
interactions.description: Roxatidine acetate can cause a decrease in the absorption of Dasatinib resulting in a reduced serum concentration and potentially a decrease in efficacy.

interactions.name: Delavirdine
interactions.description: Roxatidine acetate can cause a decrease in the absorption of Delavirdine resulting in a reduced serum concentration and potentially a decrease in efficacy.

interactions.name: Pazopanib
interactions.description: Roxatidine acetate can cause a decrease in the absorption of Pazopanib resulting in a reduced serum concentration and potentially a decrease in efficacy.

interactions.name: Risedronate
interactions.description: Roxatidine acetate can cause an increase in the absorption of Risedronate resulting in an increased serum concentration and potentially a worsening of adverse effects.

interactions.name: Ketoconazole
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Ketoconazole can be decreased when it is combined with Roxatidine acetate.

interactions.name: Mesalazine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Mesalazine can be decreased when used in combination with Roxatidine acetate.

interactions.name: Benzylpenicilloyl Polylysine
interactions.description: Roxatidine acetate may decrease effectiveness of Benzylpenicilloyl Polylysine as a diagnostic agent.

interactions.name: Hyaluronidase
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Hyaluronidase can be decreased when used in combination with Roxatidine acetate.
